goal: Bootstrap the user-provided supplier master data into the local runtime and align supplier-focused dropdowns so real suppliers can appear before assignment is complete.
deliverable: The 10 provided suppliers inserted into the current local supplier master table
Supplier-focus option derivation widened to include supplier master data
Preservation of truthful behavior: imported items remain unassigned until explicitly bound
Verification that the current baseline remains green
constraints: Do not hardcode tenant-specific supplier names into product source as defaults
Use lightweight local runtime bootstrap for the provided supplier names
Keep the round bounded to supplier usability
acceptance_criteria: The current local runtime contains the 10 supplier names the user provided
Supplier-related selection paths can see supplier master data
The supplier-analysis focus dropdown is no longer limited to assigned report rows only
product_acceptance_criteria: Users can pick real suppliers in import/manual selection flows after master data exists
The UI no longer implies that only unassigned supplier state exists when supplier master data is known
manual_spot_checks: Open an import preview and confirm supplier dropdown can show the inserted supplier names
Open supplier analysis and confirm the focus dropdown can list real suppliers
Confirm existing imported records remain unassigned until manually bound
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after supplier bootstrap succeeds and validation reruns successfully.
delivery_truth_contract: Describe only the supplier master-data bootstrap, selector alignment, and verification that actually ran.
non_goals: Hardcoded supplier seeds in application startup
inferred_assumptions: The user is providing the real supplier master list that should exist in the current local runtime.
Showing real suppliers in dropdowns is more valuable right now than attempting fragile automatic assignment.
